mechanism_narrative: Declining federal funds rates reduce corporate borrowing costs and compress equity discount rates, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns are negative in >55% of periods when FEDFUNDS declines month-over-month.

mechanism_narrative: A monthly increase in the unemployment rate exceeding 0.2 percentage points signals deteriorating labor market conditions and reduced consumer spending capacity, driving negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Forward returns exceed zero in >60% of instances when the unemployment rate crosses the 0.2pp monthly increase threshold.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous growth in retail sales and moderate inflation indicates robust consumer demand without pricing power erosion, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: The joint signal yields negative forward returns in >50% of cases when RSAFS grows >1% and CPIAUCSL growth stays below 3% annually.

mechanism_narrative: Rising industrial production index values reflect expanding manufacturing output and supply chain normalization, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: 5-day forward returns are negative in >65% of periods following a month-over-month increase in the industrial production index.

mechanism_narrative: When the consumer price index exceeds 275, market participants anticipate aggressive central bank tightening to combat inflation, compressing equity valuations and producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Forward returns remain positive in >55% of months where CPIAUCSL surpasses the 275 threshold.

mechanism_narrative: Concurrent increases in total nonfarm payrolls and decreases in the unemployment rate confirm broad-based labor market strength, supporting corporate earnings expectations and driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: The combined signal fails to predict positive returns in >50% of observation windows where both conditions are met simultaneously.

mechanism_narrative: Increasing housing starts signal strengthening residential investment and broader economic confidence, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: 60-day forward returns are negative in >60% of periods following a month-over-month increase in housing starts.

mechanism_narrative: A monthly decline in the industrial production index exceeding 0.5 percentage points indicates severe supply-side contraction and weakening economic momentum, triggering negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: 5-day forward returns exceed zero in >55% of instances when the industrial production index drops by more than 0.5% month-over-month.
